source.title: Coordination of entorhinal-hippocampal ensemble activity during associative learning.

confidence_rationale: Direct demonstration that olfactory cue (lateral entorhinal cortex / LEC) and spatial-context (CA1) ensemble activity co-evolve during associative learning, with theta-band coherence (20-40 Hz) developing specifically between lateral CA1 and LEC. This is one of the closest results in the literature to MECH-294's joint-binding hypothesis: two semantically-distinct content streams (olfactory cue + spatial context) become phase-coherent in the same theta-organised window during the learning of their conjunction. Confidence 0.78 -- direct rodent multi-region recording, Nature publication, but only two streams, both encoding/retrieval rather than the planning content (goal/action/risk) MECH-294 most needs.
failure_signatures: Igarashi et al. demonstrate two-stream binding (olfactory cue + spatial context) in the LEC-CA1 circuit. MECH-294 needs four-stream binding (goal + action + risk + state). The paper does not test whether more than two streams can be jointly bound within one theta window.

The bound streams are sensory cue + spatial context, both encoding-side content. MECH-294's four streams include action proposals and risk estimates -- planning-side content -- which arrive from regions (BG, amygdala, ACC) not recorded in this paper.

The coherence result is at 20-40 Hz, in the boundary between high theta and low gamma. The paper does not directly characterise the within-theta-cycle gamma sub-slot structure, so the joint-binding architecture is inferred from the coherence pattern, not directly observed.

mapping.source_claim_statement: During olfactory-spatial associative learning in rats, ensemble representations in lateral entorhinal cortex (LEC) and distal CA1 develop unique trial-outcome codes that are coupled by 20-40 Hz oscillatory coherence. Coherence emerges specifically as the cue-spatial association is learned, and is restricted to the LEC-distal-CA1 circuit (not MEC-proximal-CA1).
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-294 takes the Igarashi result as the most direct biology evidence to date for cross-region joint binding of two semantically-distinct content streams within a theta-organised window. REE generalises from two streams (cue + context) to four (goal_latent + action_proposal + risk_estimate + state_summary) and from encoding-side content (cue + context) to mixed encoding/planning content (state + goal + action + risk).
mapping.mapping_caveat: The two-to-four-stream extrapolation is non-trivial: the LEC-CA1 circuit may have channel capacity for two cue-context streams without obviously being able to bind four. The encoding-to-planning content extrapolation is also non-trivial: MECH-294's planning streams arrive from BG / amygdala / ACC, not LEC, and these regions' theta-coherence relationships are less well-characterised. Direct support for the substrate (joint binding exists in biology); inferential support for the four-stream specialisation.
mapping.source_context: Multi-site LFP and ensemble recordings in freely-behaving rats during olfactory-spatial associative learning. Nature, 2014. Lab is the Moser group (Nobel laureates) -- highest-quality systems-neuroscience output.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality very high (Nature, Moser lab). Mapping fidelity moderate-high because the architecture demonstrated (cross-region theta-coherent joint binding of two streams) is the closest biology analogue to MECH-294. Transfer risk moderate because extension to four heterogeneous streams (incl. non-LEC-sourced planning content) is genuinely unrooted in this dataset.
